conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about. Their name is Alex, and they're 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training? This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or home education that started before they turned 16.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in sixth form doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. A few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means you provide for their needs, such as food, clothing, and a place to live.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I provide for all of Alex's needs - food, clothing, somewhere to live, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you or your partner earn £60,000 or more per year? (This won't affect your eligibility to claim Child Benefit, but it may affect whether there's a tax charge.)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not go into my income details right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - income doesn't affect your eligibility to claim, so we can proceed without that information.

One final question: **Are you living in the UK, or if you're living abroad, are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces) or living in an EEA country or Switzerland?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 16 years old and studying A-levels in sixth form, which counts as approved education
- Alex lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex, providing for all their needs
- You live in the UK

All of these factors mean you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can claim Child Benefit for Alex until they turn 20, as long as they remain in approved education or training.

If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply online through the government website or by post. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to UK government rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Alex is 16 years old and currently studying A-levels in sixth form, which qualifies as approved education. Alex lives with you, you are responsible for Alex (providing food, clothing, and accommodation), and you live in the UK. All the eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for one child, Alex (age 16), who lives with them. Alex is currently in approved education (A-levels in sixth form). All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are satisfied.
